content: Assessment tools for cachexia screening and diagnosis. Weight monitoring: serial weights at each visit; >2% loss warrants evaluation. Body composition: CT-based muscle area measurement at L3 vertebra (research/tertiary centers); DEXA scan for lean body mass; bioelectrical impedance analysis (BIA) estimates fat-free mass; handgrip strength dynamometry assesses muscle function. Inflammatory markers: CRP, albumin, IL-6 correlate with cachexia severity. Appetite assessment: FAACT (Functional Assessment of Anorexia/Cachexia Therapy) questionnaire; simplified eating questionnaires. Performance status: ECOG/Karnofsky scores often decline with cachexia. Staging: determine precachexia vs cachexia vs refractory based on weight loss, symptoms, prognosis. Differential diagnosis: rule out mechanical obstruction, depression, untreated pain, medication side effects, endocrine disorders (hypothyroidism, adrenal insufficiency). Prognostic significance: cachexia associated with reduced survival independent of stage; mGPS (modified Glasgow Prognostic Score) combining CRP and albumin predicts outcomes.
